beats.narration: Every radiograph begins as raw film in someone's hands. A sloppy darkroom ruins a perfect exposure, and the damage mimics real flaws.

payload.title: THE DARKROOM
payload.sub: Where radiographs are made — or ruined.

beats.narration: Three rules guard the film: a safelight only slows fogging, the dry side stays divorced from the wet side, and you touch edges only.
Processing runs in a fixed sequence: develop, stop, fix, wash, dry. Development follows the time temperature chart, about five minutes at 68 degrees Fahrenheit.

payload.heading: Five steps, fixed order
payload.note: BY THE CHART — ABOUT 5 MIN AT 68 °F

beats.narration: The emulsion remembers everything: kinks print crescent crimp marks, fast sliding prints black static trees, stacked boxes print pressure marks.
So work the darkroom like it remembers: slow hands, clean bench, process by the chart, and the film shows the weld and nothing else.

payload.sub: The film shows the weld — and nothing else.
